preamble: The shift from the unrounded [ʌ] to a rounded back vowel like [o] or [ʊ] is often a conservative dialectal feature. Historically, English words with [ʌ] (like 'strut') originally had a rounded [ʊ] which unrounded in the South. In Northern dialects and some Irish varieties, this unrounding never happened, or the vowel shifted back toward a rounded quality.

examples.note: Northern English dialects lack the FOOT-STRUT split; the vowel retains historical [ʊ] rather than undergoing the southerly unrounding to [ʌ].
Dublin accents often round and raise the STRUT vowel, maintaining an older rounded quality.
